pattern: Scan the bytes left to right, decode each leading byte's length from its high bits, then require exactly that many following bytes to begin with the continuation pattern.
hints: Look at the top bits of each starting byte: they tell you how many bytes the current character should span.
After reading a leader that announces an n-byte character, the next n minus one bytes must each start with the bits one-zero.
Track how many continuation bytes you still expect; a leader resets that count, a continuation decrements it, and any mismatch or leftover expectation makes the data invalid.

edge_inputs.description: leader announces more bytes than remain
five leading ones is never a valid leader

stress.description: random bytes so most sequences are invalid in varied ways
constructed valid utf-8 streams of mixed widths
stress.note: guarantees genuinely valid encodings reach the true branch
